jokes.text: I'm slightly richer! What to do, what to do? One $300 hooker-Bot or 300 $1 hooker-Bots?
jokes.setup: Bender contemplates what to do with his tax refund money
jokes.punchline: The absurd mathematical breakdown of robot prostitute economics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character comedy - his hedonistic priorities and robot-specific concerns create a mathematically absurd but perfectly in-character dilemma

jokes.text: I'm gonna swim with a whale. They're the gentle giants of the deep. I'm well aware of that.
jokes.setup: Fry announces his whale swimming plan
jokes.punchline: The unnecessary defensive clarification about whale gentleness
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Fry's defensiveness about his simple plan, but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: Scruffy's gonna get himself one of them $300 haircuts. This one's lost its pizzazz.
jokes.setup: Scruffy considers how to spend his refund
jokes.punchline: Treating a $300 haircut as reasonable while describing his current hair as lacking 'pizzazz'
jokes.explanation: Good Scruffy character work - his oddly formal way of speaking about mundane things with unexpected vanity

jokes.text: A fortune, it is. At last, Zoidberg will live like a rich man!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg celebrates getting the tax refund
jokes.punchline: Considering $300 a fortune that will make him rich
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg poverty humor - his completely skewed perspective on money fits character perfectly

jokes.text: It's just 300 bucks. What is that, like 100 cups of coffee? That's it! I'm getting 100 cups of coffee, starting now.
jokes.setup: Fry tries to put $300 in perspective through coffee math
jokes.punchline: Immediately committing to the absurd literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Fry logic - his mathematical approach leading to impulsive commitment shows perfect character understanding and sets up his subplot

jokes.text: Show them my medal, Kif. He rented it with his tax refund.
jokes.setup: Zapp proudly asks Kif to show his medal
jokes.punchline: Kif's deadpan revelation that it's rented, not earned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp/Kif dynamic - Zapp's fraudulent grandstanding immediately deflated by Kif's matter-of-fact honesty

jokes.text: Very good, sir. Shall I pre-warm sir's crack pipe?
jokes.setup: Bender declares no more cheap crack houses
jokes.punchline: Elzar treating crack pipe warming as a normal upscale service
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the juxtaposition of high-class service language with crack addiction creates perfect absurd contrast

jokes.text: I almost feel kind of shallow for blowing my rebate on this cool talking tattoo. Hey, Gordon Gekko. I cost as much as this whole crummy date!
jokes.setup: Amy expresses guilt about her frivolous purchase
jokes.punchline: The tattoo immediately proving her point by insulting her date
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor - Amy's self-awareness about shallow spending immediately validated by the tattoo's rude behavior

jokes.text: Well, yes. In the same way an infant may fight Muhammad Ali, but-- One pound of stem cells, please!
jokes.setup: Professor tries to explain stem cells with a discouraging analogy
jokes.punchline: Amy completely ignoring the warning and ordering by weight
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy - the Professor's careful scientific explanation completely ignored for impulsive bulk purchasing

jokes.text: It was hand-rolled by Queen Elizabeth during her wild years... And was buried with George Burns until grave-robbing space mushrooms-- Well, you know the rest.
jokes.setup: Salesman explains the cigar's prestigious provenance
jokes.punchline: The explanation becoming so ridiculous he assumes everyone knows about grave-robbing space mushrooms
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - the increasingly bizarre backstory culminating in the casual assumption that grave-robbing space mushrooms are common knowledge

jokes.text: Give you 300 bucks for it. No can do. Oh, all right. I'll just take these $300 burglar's tools, then. Very good, sir. So, what time you close tonight?
jokes.setup: Bender can't afford the cigar so buys burglar's tools instead
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking closing time to plan his robbery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender criminal logic - the transition from legitimate purchase to obvious robbery planning shows his consistent character

jokes.text: Oh, all right. I'll just take these $300 burglar's tools, then. / Very good, sir. / So, what time you close tonight?
jokes.setup: Bender can't afford the cigar so buys burglar tools instead
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks closing time, revealing his obvious criminal intent to the helpful shopkeeper
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic and criminal incompetence. The shopkeeper's polite response despite obvious criminal intent makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: With a warning label this big, you know they gotta be fun.
jokes.setup: Professor points out the large warning label on the boots
jokes.punchline: Using danger warnings as a selling point for fun
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about consumer psychology - warning labels as inadvertent advertisements

jokes.text: But, Pops, I don't want to have fun. I want to be like you, boring but prudently invested.
jokes.setup: Son expresses what he wants to be like his father
jokes.punchline: Describing his father as 'boring but prudently invested' as a positive goal
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - the son wanting to emulate his father's boring financial responsibility instead of fun

jokes.text: No! I wish I had two mommies!
jokes.setup: Dwight is forced to watch his father have fun in the boots
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific wish for alternative family structure
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation - instead of wishing for normal things, he wants a specific alternative family structure to avoid this situation

jokes.text: I ask for rich-guy stuff, and you give me shiny pebbles? I bid you adieu.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is disappointed with jewelry offerings
jokes.punchline: Calling expensive jewelry 'shiny pebbles' and using formal departure language
jokes.explanation: Typical Zoidberg - his complete lack of understanding of luxury goods combined with pretentious language

jokes.text: Well, you asked the right guy. I'm the whale biologist. Though, personally, I hate whales. Especially Mushu.
jokes.setup: Introduces himself as the whale biologist
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits to hating whales, especially the one he works with
jokes.explanation: Great character introduction - the professional contradiction creates an immediately memorable and funny character

jokes.text: Then why'd you become a whale biologist? I don't know you well enough to get into that.
jokes.setup: Fry asks the logical follow-up question
jokes.punchline: The biologist treating it as too personal despite just volunteering his hatred
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - the whale biologist's arbitrary boundaries about what's too personal to share

jokes.text: Yeah. I'll have a coffee. / Guppy, trout, mermaid or--? / Whale, please.
jokes.setup: Fry orders coffee, waitress lists fish-themed sizes
jokes.punchline: Fry's choice of 'whale' size coffee, continuing his whale obsession and revealing the enormous serving
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Fry's whale theme. The fish-sized coffee portions are a good worldbuilding gag.

jokes.text: Oh! Somebody won big at Skee-Ball! You shut up!
jokes.setup: Tattoo sees Kif's romantic gift
jokes.punchline: Mocking it as a cheap arcade prize, causing Amy embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy - the tattoo's cruel accuracy about the cheap-looking gift creates social awkwardness

jokes.text: Oh! I can't wait till the tattoos on Amy's butt hear about this!
jokes.setup: Tattoo is excited about spreading gossip
jokes.punchline: Plans to tell other tattoos on Amy's body
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist concept - tattoos as a gossip network on one person's body is wonderfully ridiculous

jokes.text: Say, this reminds me of that time I ate that other watch Kif gave you.
jokes.setup: The current situation with the watch in the whale
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Bender eating a previous romantic gift
jokes.explanation: Good character callback showing Bender's consistent destructive behavior toward Kif's romantic gestures

jokes.text: The key is to grab life by the ho-joes. Live every day like it might be your last.
jokes.setup: Old man giving life advice after stem cell treatment
jokes.punchline: Mangling the phrase 'grab life by the horns' into nonsense
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay humor - the malapropism works but isn't particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Goose liver? Fish eggs? Where's the goose? Where's the fish? Hey, that's what rich people eat. The garbage parts of the food.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg questions the expensive food offerings
jokes.punchline: Accurate observation that luxury foods are often animal waste products
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor - Zoidberg's outsider perspective reveals the absurdity of expensive delicacies

jokes.text: I ate garbage yesterday, and it didn't cost me $300!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg compares expensive food to garbage
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally ate garbage for free, making it economically superior
jokes.explanation: Excellent Zoidberg logic - his poverty lifestyle actually makes him more economically rational than rich people

jokes.text: You see that? Grand-theft tobacco. Time to get off our fat cop asses and ride.
jokes.setup: Police witness Bender stealing the expensive cigar
jokes.punchline: Treating cigar theft as 'grand-theft tobacco' like car theft
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay creating a new category of crime by adapting familiar legal terminology

jokes.text: This isn't Yemeni! It's Sulawesi! The cup's shaking! I don't want my coffee shaking!
jokes.setup: Fry's increasing coffee obsession and expertise
jokes.punchline: Becoming a coffee snob while also having physical withdrawal symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good character progression showing Fry's simultaneous expertise and addiction from his coffee binge

jokes.text: No. You can't blow the smoke from such a majestic stogy in just anyone's face. I'm saving it for the fancy-pantses at Zapp Brannigan's black-tie reception.
jokes.setup: Someone urges Bender to smoke his stolen cigar
jokes.punchline: Bender's elaborate plan to blow smoke specifically at fancy people, treating rudeness as ceremonial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - treating antisocial behavior as a special occasion requiring the right target. 'Fancy-pantses' is wonderfully juvenile.

jokes.text: And the fifth reason whales kill is for the sheer fun of it. Anything else? You're lumpy and you smell awful.
jokes.setup: Biologist explaining whale behavior
jokes.punchline: Adding gratuitous personal insults to his educational lecture
jokes.explanation: Consistent character work - the whale biologist's unprofessional rudeness matches his earlier whale hatred

jokes.text: Hey, I calls them like I sees them. I'm a whale biologist.
jokes.setup: Justifying his rude behavior
jokes.punchline: Using his professional credentials to justify personal insults
jokes.explanation: Good running gag development - his professional identity becoming his excuse for everything

jokes.text: Oh, crud. He ate my suit. It was ugly! Whale biologist.
jokes.setup: The whale eating his suit
jokes.punchline: Admitting his own suit was ugly while maintaining professional authority
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - even when insulting himself, he maintains his professional signature

jokes.text: Rich people wouldn't waste their time on this nonsense. Plus, these eggs are gritty and tasteless.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg critiques his expensive meal experience
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the point of luxury while accurately describing caviar
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg character work - his outsider perspective accidentally delivers accurate food criticism

jokes.text: Come on, Mushu! Barf! Barf like a freshman!
jokes.setup: Fry desperately needs the whale to vomit up the watch
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific college reference for vomiting
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor connecting whale vomiting to college drinking culture

jokes.text: Better do what he says. He's a whale biologist.
jokes.setup: Police want to arrest Fry for stealing aquarium property
jokes.punchline: Fry deferring to the whale biologist's authority in all matters
jokes.explanation: Running gag payoff - the whale biologist's authority extending beyond his actual expertise

jokes.text: Crazy bugs actually wove this tapestry of my heroic conquest while I was still killing them.
jokes.setup: Zapp describes the Spidarian tapestry
jokes.punchline: Reveals the aliens wove artwork of their own defeat while being massacred, which Zapp finds impressive rather than horrific
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and perfectly Zapp. The image of aliens documenting their own genocide while it happens is horrifically funny, and Zapp's casual pride makes it even darker.

jokes.text: What? It's not even scratch and sniff.
jokes.setup: Bender examines the billion-dollar tapestry
jokes.punchline: Judging priceless art by lack of novelty features
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender perspective - reducing high art to its lack of gimmicky features shows his materialistic worldview

jokes.text: But if rich people think it's good, I'll buy it. One art, please!
jokes.setup: Bender decides the art must be good based on price
jokes.punchline: Ordering art like a commodity with the phrase 'one art'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - 'One art, please!' perfectly captures Bender's mechanical approach to culture and became a widely quoted line

jokes.text: What a clever impersonation of a stupid poor person.
jokes.setup: Rich person observes Bender's behavior
jokes.punchline: Assuming his genuine ignorance is performance art
jokes.explanation: Sharp class commentary - the rich person's inability to recognize genuine working-class behavior

jokes.text: The only way to keep the butler from running away is to cut off his foot.
jokes.setup: Rich woman tells what she thinks is a funny story
jokes.punchline: Casually describes maiming servants as household management advice
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy about wealthy sociopathy. The casual tone makes the horrific content even funnier.

jokes.text: Yes, it reminds me of a joke I heard about upper-middle-class people.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg tries to relate to rich people's conversation
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of social class hierarchies
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg character moment showing his social obliviousness and misplaced aspirations

jokes.text: Jail's not so bad. You can make sangria in the toilet. Of course, it's shank or be shanked.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to comfort Amy about Kif being in jail
jokes.punchline: Her comforting words immediately contradicted by prison violence reality
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy structure - the attempt at comfort immediately undercut by harsh reality

jokes.text: Precious hamburgers?
jokes.setup: Whale biologist mentions 'precious ambergris'
jokes.punchline: Kif's innocent mishearing of the word
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay humor that fits Kif's innocent character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: This has been Roseanne, your guide to the world of facts.
jokes.setup: Educational definition of ambergris
jokes.punchline: Roseanne Barr as an unlikely educational narrator
jokes.explanation: Amusing absurdist choice - using Roseanne as an educational authority is unexpected but not deeply funny

jokes.text: Or better yet, I'll simply shed my skin.
jokes.setup: Kif needs to remove the valuable ambergris coating
jokes.punchline: His alien ability to shed skin as a practical solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent use of Kif's alien biology for practical problem-solving - very species-specific humor

jokes.text: You, wrinkled as a prune. You, fat as the queen of sea cows. I love you!
jokes.setup: Both characters reveal their true appearances
jokes.punchline: Expressing love immediately after brutal honest descriptions
jokes.explanation: Good romantic comedy structure - brutal honesty leading to genuine acceptance and love

jokes.text: Using that, I'll make you a perfume of lilac and jasmine and frankenberry.
jokes.setup: Kif promises to make perfume from the ambergris
jokes.punchline: Including breakfast cereal as a romantic fragrance component
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist touch - mixing elegant perfume ingredients with cartoon cereal shows Kif's sweet naivety

jokes.text: Who smells like freaking porpoise hork? I do!
jokes.setup: Amy excitedly puts on the 'perfume' made from whale vomit
jokes.punchline: Her enthusiastic self-identification with the horrible smell
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Amy's proud announcement of smelling terrible creates awkward humor

jokes.text: Ah. So now I'm in the gutter, surrounded by bums who eat garbage? Money brought me no happiness. Bupkis.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg realizes his situation after spending his money
jokes.punchline: Not realizing he was already eating garbage before having money
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Zoidberg's earlier garbage eating - his lack of self-awareness about his unchanged situation

jokes.text: Everyone, to the fire door! It's on fire!
jokes.setup: Emergency evacuation announcement
jokes.punchline: The escape route is also on fire
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - the emergency exit being part of the problem creates perfectly illogical comedy

jokes.text: Coffee, coffee, coffee!
jokes.setup: Fry is completely wired from 100 cups of coffee during the fire
jokes.punchline: His manic chanting while presumably moving at super speed to save everyone
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the coffee subplot. The manic energy and single-minded focus perfectly captures extreme caffeine effects.

jokes.text: I think we were saved by a mysterious orange blur.
jokes.setup: Characters trying to understand how they escaped the fire
jokes.punchline: Describing Fry's coffee-fueled super-speed as a mysterious phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Fry's coffee subplot - his hyperactivity becoming heroic from others' perspective

jokes.text: Oysters Rockefeller here has provided genuine turkey dogs.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg introduces the feast provider
jokes.punchline: Giving someone a fancy nickname for providing cheap hot dogs
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg character work - his misplaced formality and gratitude for simple generosity

jokes.text: I'm gonna take this and buy five shares of Amazon.com. A risk-taker. That's my boy.
jokes.setup: Dwight plans to invest his penny
jokes.punchline: His father calling tiny stock investment 'risk-taking' in the dot-com era
jokes.explanation: Good period humor about dot-com era investment attitudes, works better in hindsight

jokes.text: Why did I have to issue that crooked tax rebate? At least we got a few mildly interesting stories out of it.
jokes.setup: Nixon regrets his policy decision
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on the episode's anthology structure being 'mildly interesting'
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor acknowledging the episode format while staying in character for Nixon

jokes.text: Oh, what a McGovern I've been.
jokes.setup: Nixon realizes his tax rebate policy failed
jokes.punchline: Uses 'McGovern' as an insult/self-criticism, referencing his political rival
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nixon characterization using political rival's name as insult. Shows his competitive nature and historical awareness.

jokes.text: Speaking of which, my story kind of petered out... Without me learning a lesson. There he is. All right! Closure!
jokes.setup: Bender notes his story lacked resolution
jokes.punchline: Police immediately arriving to provide artificial closure
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy - Bender's self-awareness about narrative structure immediately resolved by convenient plot device

jokes.text: Futurama. Noun. I don't know. You just watched it, dummy. What are you asking me for? Hey, here's a fun definition. Idiot. Noun. You! This has been Roseanne, your guide to the world of facts.
jokes.setup: Educational closing segment about the show itself
jokes.punchline: Roseanne breaking character to insult the audience directly
jokes.explanation: Good meta-callback - Roseanne's educational persona breaking down into direct audience hostility

jokes.text: 'Futurama'. Noun. I don't know. You just watched it, dummy. What are you asking me for?
jokes.setup: Roseanne returns as the facts narrator for the show's title
jokes.punchline: Her hostile response to defining the show we just watched, breaking fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta ending. Roseanne's hostile refusal to explain the show title while acknowledging the audience just watched it is perfectly subversive.
